preamble: The voiceless alveolo-palatal affricate (t͡ɕ) can undergo a phonetic transformation to its voiced counterpart, the voiced alveolo-palatal affricate (d͡ʑ). This shift is a form of lenition, where a voiceless sound becomes voiced, often influenced by surrounding phonetic environments.

examples.note: Free variation in the voicing of the unaspirated sibilant affricate, especially before high front vowels. Specific lexical examples are not provided in the source.

sources: Lotven, S. A. (2021). The Sound Systems of Zophei Dialects and Other Maraic Languages. Indiana University.
